title: Lisowskia welwitschii
taxonomy: Lisowskia welwitschii is a genus of flowering plants in the family Brassicaceae. It is a monotypic genus, meaning it contains only one species, Lisowskia welwitschii. The species is native to southern Africa, specifically in the countries of South Africa and Mozambique. Lisowskia welwitschii is classified as a crucifer, a group of plants that includes mustard, cabbage, and broccoli, due to its characteristic four-petaled flowers. Further classification of the species may be made using more specific botanical keys.

intro: Lisowskia welwitschii, also known as Lisowskia, is a plant species belonging to the family Orobanchaceae, characterized by its parasitic nature, where it relies on other plants for its nutritional needs. Traditionally, Lisowskia welwitschii has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties.
